title: Codonoboea pauziana
taxonomy: Codonoboea pauziana belongs to the family Gesneriaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is a type of epiphyte, which means it grows on other plants for support. The classification of Codonoboea pauziana includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Magnoliophyta, and class as Magnoliopsida. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in Borneo. Its exact classification may be further detailed through more specific taxonomic information.

The health benefits of Codonoboea pauziana include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
